title: Topological Hochschild homology and integral $p$-adic Hodge theory

thm:1.2.summary: Existence of $\mathfrak{S}$-linear cohomology theory $R\Gamma_{\mathfrak{S}}(\mathfrak{X})$ with $\varphi$-linear Frobenius, recovering $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-cohomology after base change. The Breuil–Kisin upgrade of BMS1's $A\Omega$.

thm:1.6.summary: For $R$ perfectoid, $\pi_0 \mathrm{TC}^-(R; \mathbb{Z}_p) \cong A_{\mathrm{inf}}(R)$ canonically and $\varphi$-equivariantly. Identifies $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$ with a topological Hochschild invariant — the bridge from $p$-adic Hodge to homotopy theory.

thm:1.8.summary: For $A$ a $p$-adic completion of a smooth $\mathcal{O}_C$-algebra, $A\Omega_A \simeq R\Gamma_{\mathrm{syn}}(A, \pi_0 \mathrm{TC}^-(-; \mathbb{Z}_p))$ as $E_\infty$-$A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-algebras with $\varphi$. Realizes $A\Omega$ as a quasisyntomic-site sheaf cohomology of $\mathrm{TC}^-$.

thm:1.10.summary: For perfect $k$ of char $p$ and $A/k$ smooth: $R\Gamma_{\mathrm{crys}}(A/W(k)) \simeq R\Gamma_{\mathrm{syn}}(A, \pi_0 \mathrm{TC}^-(-; \mathbb{Z}_p))$ as $E_\infty$-$W(k)$-algebras with Frobenius. Char-$p$ specialization recovering crystalline.

thm:1.12.summary: Motivic filtration: for $A$ quasisyntomic, locally on $\mathrm{qSyn}_A$ the spectra $\mathrm{THH}, \mathrm{TC}^-, \mathrm{TP}$ are concentrated in even degrees, and $\mathrm{Fil}^n \mathrm{THH}(A; \mathbb{Z}_p) = R\Gamma_{\mathrm{syn}}(A, \tau_{\ge 2n})$ (and similarly for $\mathrm{TC}^-, \mathrm{TP}$) gives a complete decreasing motivic filtration.

thm:1.15.summary: Cohomological identifications: for smooth $A/k$ in char $p$, $\mathbb{Z}_p(n) \simeq W\Omega^n_{X, \log}[-n]$ on the pro-étale site of $\mathrm{Spec}(A)$. Realizes the BMS2 motivic-filtration sheaves as classical objects (logarithmic de Rham–Witt).

thm:1.17.summary: Relative motivic filtration: for $R \in \mathrm{QSyn}$ and $A \in \mathrm{qSyn}_R$, complete exhaustive multiplicative filtrations on $\mathrm{HC}^-(A/R)$ with graded pieces controlled by $\tau_{\ge 2n}$. The relative analog of thm:1.12.

thm:3.1.summary: fpqc-sheaf property: $A \mapsto \wedge^i_A L_{A/R}$ is an fpqc sheaf with values in $D(R)$. The descent property that licenses building cohomology theories by quasisyntomic sheafification.

thm:5.4.summary: Beilinson $t$-structure on $DF(R)$: a symmetric-monoidal $t$-structure with explicit connective cover. The general homological-algebra device used to extract motivic filtrations from filtered objects.

thm:6.1.summary: $\pi_* \mathrm{THH}(R; \mathbb{Z}_p) = R[u]$ is polynomial in degree $2$, with generator $u \in \ker(\theta)/\ker(\theta)^2$. The local computation that powers thm:1.6.

thm:7.1.summary: For $S \in \mathrm{QRSPerfd}_R$, $\pi_* \mathrm{THH}(S; \mathbb{Z}_p)$ is concentrated in even degrees and multiplication by $u$ gives a natural injection $\pi_{2i-2} \to \pi_{2i}$. Even-degree concentration on quasiregular semiperfectoid rings.

thm:7.2.summary: On quasiregular semiperfectoids, the homotopy-fixed-point and Tate spectral sequences for $\mathrm{TC}^-, \mathrm{TP}$ degenerate; both are concentrated in even degrees with explicit canonical map $\pi_*. The convergence input to the motivic filtration.

thm:7.15.summary: Clausen–Mathew–Morrow: the trace $K(S; \mathbb{Z}_p) \to \tau_{\ge 0} \mathrm{TC}(S; \mathbb{Z}_p)$ is an equivalence for $S$ Henselian along $pS$ with $S/pS$ semiperfect. The $K$-theory side of the motivic filtration.

thm:8.14.summary: For $S$ quasiregular semiperfect: $\mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{crys}}(S)$ is $p$-torsion-free and $\varphi_i \bmod p: \mathcal{N}^i \mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{crys}} \to \mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{crys}}/p$ is injective with image $\mathrm{Fil}_i^{\mathrm{conj}}$. Char-$p$ specialization machinery.

thm:9.6.summary: For smooth $A/\mathcal{O}_C$ ($p$-adic completion thereof), $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_A \simeq A\Omega_A$ as $E_\infty$-$A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-algebras compatibly with Frobenius. Identifies the BMS2 $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}$ with BMS1's $A\Omega$ — the bridge between the two papers.

thm:10.1.summary: Pro-étale truncation: $\mathbb{Z}/p^n \mathbb{Z}(i) \simeq \tau^{\le i} R\psi_* \mathbb{Z}/p^n \mathbb{Z}(i)$ for $\psi: X_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \to \mathfrak{X}_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$. Connects motivic-filtration sheaves to nearby-cycles cohomology — key bridge to thm:1.15.

thm:11.2.summary: Construction of the Breuil–Kisin module $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_{A/\mathfrak{S}}$: a $(p,z)$-complete $E_\infty$-algebra over $\mathfrak{S}$ with $\varphi$-linear Frobenius, functorial in smooth affine formal $A/\mathcal{O}_K$. Body construction underlying thm:1.2.

edges.note: Existence of the $\mathfrak{S}$-cohomology theory is the body construction.
Polynomial structure of $\mathrm{THH}$ is the local computation underlying $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$ identification.
Even-degree concentration on perfectoids comes from the QRSPerfd computation.
Motivic filtration uses even-degree concentration to define the syntomic-site filtration.
fpqc-sheaf property of cotangent powers is the descent licence.
Pro-étale truncation realizes $\mathbb{Z}_p(n)$ as the motivic-filtration sheaf.
Char-$p$ identification supplies the de Rham–Witt logarithmic input.
Relative version of the motivic filtration, with $R$ as the base.
Even-degree concentration uses the polynomial $\mathrm{THH}$ of perfectoid base.
Breuil–Kisin construction gives the $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}$ side of the equivalence.
$A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-recovery anchors the construction at the perfectoid base.
Gives the $K$-theoretic interpretation of the motivic filtration.
Crystalline data on quasiregular semiperfect rings parallels the topological side.

main_results.description: Section 1. Statements of the Breuil–Kisin upgrade ($\mathfrak{S}$-cohomology), $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$ as $\mathrm{TC}^-$, $A\Omega$ as a syntomic-site cohomology, motivic filtration on $\mathrm{THH/TC^-/TP}$, and the cohomological identification $\mathbb{Z}_p(n) \simeq W\Omega^n_{X,\log}[-n]$.

homotopy_theoretic_machinery.description: Sections 5–7. Beilinson $t$-structure, $\mathrm{THH}$ polynomial structure on perfectoids, even-degree concentration on QRSPerfd, $\mathrm{TC}^-/\mathrm{TP}$ degeneration, Clausen–Mathew–Morrow trace.

descent_and_crystalline_input.description: Sections 3, 8. fpqc descent of $\wedge^i L_{A/R}$ powering the syntomic-sheaf framework; quasiregular semiperfect crystalline machinery.

BMS1_identification_and_pro_etale_trunca.description: Sections 9, 10. Identification of $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}$ with $A\Omega$; pro-étale truncation realizing the motivic-filtration sheaves.

Breuil_Kisin_module.description: Section 11. Construction of the BK module $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_{A/\mathfrak{S}}$.

BMS.title: Integral p-adic Hodge theory
BMS.role: Predecessor; thm:9.6 identifies BMS2's $\widehat{\mathbbl{\Delta}}$ with BMS1's $A\Omega$.

CMM.title: Algebraic K-theory and descent for blow-ups (or the cyclotomic trace)

Beilinson:tStructure.title: On the derived category of perverse sheaves (Beilinson $t$-structure)
